summary: Do not ship the plan: the RED lock and production call-site witnesses are sound, but Round 16’s sealed `absence.rs`/single-tally design conflicts with normative pseudocode. Simpler alternative: remove `Vanished` and the guard and only skip confirmed source absence; already rejected because an unrecreated `.objects` loss would appear to be a clean GC pass. Open question: none.

findings.title: The grave pseudocode still uses the pre-relocation `atomic` API
findings.body: The normative snippet references `atomic::Absent`, `atomic::Vanished`, `atomic::rename_durable_source_checked`, and `atomic::Renamed`, although Round 16 moves them to `reconcile/absence.rs` and leaves `atomic.rs` with ordinary durability helpers. It also repeats the withdrawn claim that omitting one bump is inexpressible. Following this text either fails compilation or encourages moving the types back into `atomic`, undoing the sealed visibility boundary and allowing alternate tallies.
findings.recommendation: Replace every live `atomic::` reference with one explicit `reconcile::absence` import/re-export map, including the helper needed by `pins.rs`; update W5’s location; and remove the stale M-COUNT claim in favor of the two M-NOBUMP behavioral killers.

findings.title: The concrete `PassGuard::begin` diff omits the sealed pass tally
findings.body: This diff still calls `recover_graves` without `&Vanished`, contradicting the required signature at lines 699–703 and the single tally created at lines 649–652. The stale behavior row at line 780 likewise says a grave-loop disappearance leaves `vanished == 0` and skips the guard. An implementer following these instructions either hits a signature mismatch or recreates the discarded separate-tally behavior.
findings.recommendation: Show `PassGuard::begin(..., vanished: &Vanished)` passing that same reference to `recover_graves(..., vanished)`, and correct line 780 to state that the tally is positive, the guard runs, and it accepts the recreated directory.

next_steps: Normalize all live pseudocode and behavior tables to the post-Round-16 module and tally design.
Repeat the plan review after these document-only corrections; the committed RED record need not be regenerated.
